blocks.content: After the crying. After the quiet that followed it.
He sat back up. Wiped his face. Didn't apologize for any of it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: From your side. I want to hear it from you.

So he told her. The dinner table. The way she stopped mid-sentence. How fast it happened — the color draining from her face, the chair, the floor. What his hands had felt like on the way around the table. What her face had looked like.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Lena called 911. She's the one who kept it together.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: He's out there. They all are. Your mom, Maya, Nathan. Lena.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Tell my dad first. Tell him I'm okay.
She was quiet for a moment. He waited.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I thought — for a moment I thought I was watching you—

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I need you to understand that for me, in those six minutes, everything I—
He stopped again. Looked at the window. The dark glass. His own reflection in it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You are every good thing in my life. You understand that? Everything good that I have is because of you and I don't say it enough and I should say it more.
She reached up and touched his face. Her hand against his cheek.
He turned his face into her hand for just a second. Then he straightened.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm going to go tell your dad.
He stood. He was halfway to the door.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm always going to come back.
